mnemonics: CPU, RAM, and Disk, make the app's energy brisk. (Remember the three main resources that consume energy: Central Processing Unit, Random Access Memory, and Disk Storage.)
keyPoints: Measure energy consumption at the component level (CPU, RAM, storage, network) to identify hotspots.
Use profiling tools specifically designed for energy measurement, not just performance benchmarks.
Consider both active energy use and the energy cost of idle resources during low activity.
commonMistakes: Assuming that a faster execution time always equates to lower energy consumption (it often does, but not if it uses more powerful, energy-intensive hardware).
Overlooking the energy consumption of the entire system stack, including cooling and peripheral devices, focusing only on the application server.
